title: ferray-linalg: matmul/dot use naive O(n^3) loops — not using faer/BLAS for matrix multiply
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

matmul's 2D x 2D path in products/mod.rs (the matmul_2d function) and the matmul shortcut in einsum both use a hand-written triple loop: for i..m { for p..k { for j..n { c[i*n+j] += a[i*k+p] * b[p*n+j] } } }. Similarly, mat_mul_flat in solve.rs (used by matrix_power) is a naive triple loop. NumPy delegates matrix multiplication to BLAS dgemm/sgemm which is highly optimized (cache-blocking, SIMD, multi-threaded). faer also has optimized matmul. These hot loops should delegate to faer's matmul or link against BLAS, not use scalar triple loops. For a 1000x1000 matmul, this is the difference between ~0.1s (BLAS) and ~10s (naive).
